topic: Read tool — examples and best practices in AI agent harnesses

sources.title: Best Practices for Claude Code
keyInsights: Context window fills fast; performance degrades as it fills
Use Grep/Glob to locate, Read to inspect, subagents to offload
Named failure pattern: 'infinite exploration' (unbounded reading)
Use `/clear` between unrelated tasks
Reference files with `@` or let Claude fetch itself via Bash/MCP/Read

sources.title: Explore the context window
keyInsights: Interactive walkthrough of how startup prompts, MCP tools, and file reads consume the 200k window
MCP tool schemas are deferred by default; loaded on demand via tool_search
Every Read permanently bills against context
Status lines can track live token usage

sources.title: Tool use with Claude (overview)
keyInsights: Client tools vs server tools: where execution happens
Anthropic-schema tools (bash, text_editor) are trained-in
tool_use / tool_result contract
Strict tool use enforces schema conformance

sources.title: How tool use works
keyInsights: while stop_reason == tool_use loop
Server-executed tools can pause_turn when hitting iteration cap
Anthropic-schema tools outperform custom equivalents for the same operation
Tool use fits for side effects, fresh data, structured outputs, external-system calls

sources.title: Building Effective Agents
keyInsights: Spent effort on ACI (agent-computer interface) design comparable to HCI
Concrete lesson: switched to absolute filepaths after model errors with relative paths
Poka-yoke: make wrong inputs impossible via the schema
Give tool definitions as much prompt engineering attention as prompts

sources.title: Create custom subagents
keyInsights: Subagents run in their own context window
Can be tool-scoped (e.g. Read/Grep/Glob only) to enforce read-only behavior
Preserve main context by delegating large file reads
Description field drives when Claude delegates

sources.title: Claude Code overview
keyInsights: Claude Code reads codebase, edits files, runs commands
Works in terminal, IDE, desktop, web
CLAUDE.md loaded every session; files referenced via @

sources.title: Claude Code settings
keyInsights: Permission scopes: Managed / User / Project / Local
Tool access controlled via settings.json
Read tool permissions can be allowlisted per scope

sources.title: Aider repo map
keyInsights: Repo map gives a concise index of classes/functions across the repo
Graph-ranking algorithm selects relevant portions within --map-tokens budget (default 1k)
LLM uses the map to request files via /add
Aider philosophy: proactive curation, not reactive reads

sources.title: Aider commands (/add, /read-only, /drop)
keyInsights: /add makes files editable; /read-only for reference
/drop frees context tokens
/ls lists currently included files
Editable vs read-only distinction is core to Aider's model

sources.title: Aider tips
keyInsights: Only add files you think need editing
Pre-create new files with /add before editing
Paste specific error outputs, not full logs
Thoughtful curation beats comprehensive file dumps

sources.title: Aider base_coder.py (source)
keyInsights: Files wrapped in fence markers with relative path prefix
Line numbers exist in code but commented out by default
Images handled via base64 encoding
Read-only files use separate get_read_only_files_content()

sources.title: Cline tools guide
keyInsights: read_file complements replace_in_file for precise edits
Use read_file for full content; search_files for regex patterns
Implicit pattern: read first, modify second

sources.title: Cline Plan vs Act modes
keyInsights: Plan Mode: read + search only, no writes
Small tasks skip planning; medium use Plan selectively; large use /deep-planning
Enable Checkpoints before Act for rollback
Start in Plan mode for unfamiliar code

sources.title: Cline prompt engineering guide
keyInsights: Explicit file paths trigger context-based rules reliably
Multi-layer context evaluator: mentioned paths, open tabs, visible panes, session history
Conditional rules via glob patterns conserve context budget
200k token budget constraint drives design

sources.title: OpenCode tools
keyInsights: read tool supports line-range partial reads
Permission system: allow/deny/ask per tool
Default ripgrep-style .gitignore filtering
Combine read with grep/glob for discovery

sources.title: OpenCode agents
keyInsights: Tool access via permission system (allow/deny/ask)
Plan mode restricts writes/bash; Build has all tools
Explore subagent is fast, read-only
Last matching rule takes precedence in permission rules

sources.title: OpenCode rules / AGENTS.md loading
keyInsights: AGENTS.md preloaded via upward directory traversal
Precedence: local AGENTS.md → global ~/.config/opencode/AGENTS.md → CLAUDE.md compatibility
instructions field in opencode.json supports glob patterns
Lazy-load external files when possible

sources.title: Continue readFile tool definition (source)
keyInsights: Tool name: readFile (BuiltInToolNames.ReadFile)
Single parameter: filepath (relative/absolute/tilde/file:// URI)
Properties: readOnly, instant execution, allowed by default
evaluateFileAccessPolicy enforces workspace boundary

sources.title: OpenAI Agents SDK tools
keyInsights: FileSearchTool retrieves from OpenAI Vector Stores (Responses models only)
Parameters: vector_store_ids, max_num_results, filters, ranking_options
Custom file-reading via @function_tool decorator
Pair FileSearch with max_num_results to limit context

gaps: Could not fetch official Codex CLI reference (403/404) — Codex details are synthesized from repo + docs fragments
Continue's full built-in tools page was 404 on docs site; relied on source definition file
No direct coverage of Cursor/Windsurf/Zed AI read tools
